metadata.title: Octafuse Gateway · Admin
metadata.description: Operator console for Octafuse Gateway: API keys, upstream providers, models, routes, and analytics. Licensed under AGPLv3.

common.name: Name
common.billingCurrencyMustBeUsdOrCny: Billing currency must be USD or CNY
common.bodyMustBeJsonObject: Body must be a JSON object

keys.secretBanner: New API key — copy it now; the full secret will not be shown again.

providers.title: Providers
providers.subtitle: Upstream AI endpoints consumed by {product}
providers.importHint: Use Import to seed common provider endpoints. LLM-only templates use a chat URL (not Base); full OpenAI-capable ones keep a Base URL. Then open a row and add an API key.

import.title: Import from templates
import.subtitle: Prefills common endpoints. LLM-only OpenAI templates import chat URLs; providers with Images keep a Base URL. Each import creates a new provider row.

modal.prefilledFrom: Pre-filled from {id}. Set a new ID and review fields before saving.
modal.generalHint: Display name and optional custom ID (new providers only).
modal.idHint: Leave empty to auto-generate a UUID
modal.endpointsHint: One base URL per protocol for full capabilities. Expand overrides for non-standard URLs, or clear Base and fill only supported overrides to limit capabilities.
modal.geminiHint: Full path prefix before {model}. Developer: …/v1beta/models; Vertex Express: …/v1/publishers/google/models.
modal.advancedHint: Optional full URLs (supports {model}). With a Base URL, blank fields still derive from Base (all capabilities). To limit capabilities (e.g. chat only), clear Base and fill only the overrides you support.
modal.descriptionHint: Internal reference only; not sent to upstream.

keys.label: Label
keys.leaveBlank: Leave blank to keep current key

models.title: Models
models.subtitle: Maintain the models {product} exposes to clients. Seed rows from the built-in catalog with Import, or add definitions manually with New.
models.emptyHint: Import from the built-in catalog or create a model manually

imagePricing.hint: Legacy per-image catalog pricing was removed. Configure Image token rates instead.
imagePricing.disabledHint: Per-image billing is not supported; configure image_* token rates.

import.title: Import from Static Catalog
import.subtitle: Presets already in the gateway (same model id) are disabled and are never overwritten on import — delete then re-import (or edit & save) to pick up gpt-image-2 token rates. Prices use the catalog's {currency} branch (USD/CNY; see {unit}).
import.searchPlaceholder: Search model ID or display name

modal.kindHintLlm: Chat / completion models with token pricing (input / output / cache).
modal.kindHintImage: Image-generation models: output includes image, Image token rates, no context/max tokens.
modal.imageNoTokenLimits: Image models do not use chat context window or max output tokens — those fields are cleared on save.
modal.tagsPlaceholder: e.g. free, lite, pro, max — press Enter to add

card.imageEstimateHint: Estimate from Image Output × quality/size tokens — not billed. Actual charge uses upstream Images usage (Text Input + Image Input/Output).
card.imageFallbackTitle: Advanced fallback (used only if matrix cell misses)

card.clickToAdd: Click + to add the first route

protocol.stickyOn: Sticky key routing on · idle TTL {ttl}s · short wait {wait}ms (click to configure)
protocol.stickyOff: Sticky key routing off (click to configure)

listItem.chargedTooltip: Charged factor: {value} · customer billing multiplier vs catalog price
listItem.meteredTooltip: Metered factor: {value} · provider cost multiplier vs catalog price

sticky.title: Sticky key routing
sticky.enableHint: Bind each user to one provider key for this protocol × group to maximize upstream prompt-cache hits. The binding is released after the idle TTL.
sticky.shortWaitHint: Short wait: if the bound key is briefly rate-limited and expected to recover within this window, the gateway waits instead of switching keys (preserves the cache).

modal.prefilledFrom: Pre-filled from route {id}. Review fields before saving.
modal.protocolHintConfigured: Only protocols with a configured base URL on this provider.
modal.routeGroupHint: Routing pool for client modelId:group. Omit :group → default only. No active routes → 400.
modal.priorityHint: Higher = tried first within the same protocol group.
modal.standardCatalogHint: Read-only catalog from pricing_profile. Runtime = catalog × factor × schedule.
modal.standardCatalogHintImage: Token rates from pricing_profile (upstream Images usage). Expand for quality×size estimate only — not billed.
modal.noCatalogPricing: This model has no catalog pricing_profile.
modal.noCatalogImagePricing: This image model has no image token rates (tier image_* prices).
modal.selectModelForTiers: Select a model to load catalog tiers.
modal.imageResolveHint: Charge authority: upstream Images usage × tier token rates.
modal.imageTokenResolveHint: Charge authority: upstream Images usage × tier token rates.
modal.imageEstimateHint: Estimate from Image Output × quality/size tokens — not billed. Actual charge uses upstream Images usage (Text Input + Image Input/Output).
modal.chargedFactorTitle: Multiplier vs catalog standard price for charged_cost. Default 1.
modal.meteredFactorTitle: Multiplier vs catalog standard price for metered_cost. Default 1.
modal.billingTimezoneHint: Schedule windows use business timezone {timezone} (half-open [start, end); end may be 24:00).
modal.scheduleEmpty: No windows — schedule factor stays 1 all day.
modal.requestDefaultsHint: Deep-merged into upstream body; client fields win.

playground.title: Playground
playground.subtitle: {product} — send one upstream request for a single model route. No API key billing, no api_key_request_logs, no failover.
playground.chooseRouteHint: Choose a route above to see catalog fields.
playground.sentBodyHint: (server merges custom_params and writes model plus other upstream-aligned JSON fields)
playground.requestTargetUrlEmpty: Select a route to preview the full upstream URL.
playground.requestTargetUrlHint: Preview from provider base_url (same join rules as Send). After Send, shows the URL actually used.
playground.emptyResponseHint: Run Send to see upstream status, headers, and body or stream output here.
playground.imageOpenaiOnly: Image-generation models only work with upstream_protocol=openai (Playground calls /images/generations or /images/edits). Edit the route or pick an openai route.
playground.imageGenerationsHint: Image model: request body uses images/generations fields (prompt, n, size, quality). No billing or request logs.
playground.imageEditsHint: Image edits: JSON fields (prompt, n, size, quality) plus reference image files below. Files are sent as multipart to the upstream edits endpoint. No billing or request logs.
playground.referenceImagesHint: Select 1–{max} image file(s). Sent as multipart (not in the JSON editor).
playground.referenceImagesRequired: At least one reference image is required.

simulator.title: Simulator
simulator.subtitle: {product} — Browser calls the Proxy directly, like a real client: auth, route groups, failover, billing, and api_key_request_logs.
simulator.usageNote: Usage below is informational only. The API key stays in memory and is not written to localStorage.
simulator.localDevHint: Local dev example: http://127.0.0.1:8787 — avoid pointing at production by mistake.
simulator.routeGroupHint: From active routes for this model. OpenAI/Anthropic set body.model to id or id:group; Gemini uses the same path segment.
simulator.matchingRoutesNeedModel: Select a model to preview routes that would match.
simulator.matchingRoutesEmpty: No active routes match this model and route group.
simulator.keysLimitHint: — narrow filters to see more within the scroll limit.
simulator.requestTargetUrlEmpty: Complete Proxy Base URL, model, and API key to preview the full URL.
simulator.emptyResponseHint: After you click Send, status, body, or streamed output appears here.
simulator.readyNeedProxyUrl: Enter a valid Proxy Base URL (http/https).
simulator.readyNeedOpenaiForImage: Image-generation models require protocol openai (Proxy /v1/images/generations or /v1/images/edits).
simulator.readyNeedKey: Select an API key and wait until it loads (sk-…).
simulator.protocolLockedImage: Image models are locked to openai → POST /v1/images/generations or /v1/images/edits.
simulator.imageGenerationsHint: Generations: JSON body for POST /v1/images/generations (prompt, n, size, quality).
simulator.imageEditsHint: Edits: JSON fields plus reference image files → multipart POST /v1/images/edits.
simulator.referenceImagesHint: Select 1–{max} image file(s) for multipart upload.
simulator.referenceImagesRequired: At least one reference image is required.
simulator.protocolSwitchConfirm: Request body was edited. Switch protocol and replace it with the default template?
simulator.wirePreviewEmpty: Complete Proxy URL, model, and API key with valid JSON to preview the request.
simulator.errProxyUrlInvalid: Invalid Proxy Base URL (must be http:// or https://)
simulator.errSelectKey: Select an API key and wait until it finishes loading (sk-…)
simulator.errBodyMustBeObject: Request body must be a JSON object
simulator.infoModelOverwritten: Set request body model to "{model}" (matches selected model and route group).

users.title: Users
users.subtitle: Gateway users (users) — budgets and external identity

help.createUser: Creates a new gateway user. Email is required; all other fields are optional. To link this user to an account in another system, use the External identity section at the bottom.
help.budgetMax: Maximum spendable amount in the current cycle. Leave empty for unlimited.
help.budgetBase: Reference amount used to reset Budget max when the budget cycle resets.
help.budgetSpent: Amount already spent in the current cycle. Edit only to manually adjust.
help.budgetPeriod: Reset cycle for spent / Budget max. none disables auto-reset.
help.budgetResetAt: Next time spent (and Budget max from base) will auto-reset. Ignored when Budget period is none.
help.metadataReplace: Saving replaces the entire metadata object. Leave empty to keep current value unchanged.
help.disabledUser: Disabled users cannot use any API key for new requests.

externalIdentity.title: External identity
externalIdentity.hint: Use these fields to link this user to an account in another system (e.g. your own SaaS, an OAuth provider). Leave both empty for an internal Gateway-only user. Both fields must be set together or both left blank.
externalIdentity.createHint: Use these fields to link this user to an account in another system (e.g. your own SaaS, an OAuth provider). Leave both empty for an internal Gateway-only user. When both are set, creation is idempotent on the (external system, external user ID) pair.

metadata.invalidRaw: Stored value is not valid JSON; showing raw string.

confirm.deleteUser: Permanently delete this user and all keys? Audit history will remain (user link cleared).
confirm.deleteKey: Permanently delete this key from the database?

keysPage.title: API Keys
keysPage.subtitle: Manage user API keys for gateway access

create.existingHelp1: Enter the gateway User ID (users.id from the Users list). Prefer creating keys from a user's detail page when you are already there.
create.existingHelp2: Multiple active keys per user are allowed. Budget lives on the user — edit under Users.
create.secretOnce: The full sk-… secret is shown once in the banner above the list; copy it immediately.
create.metadataHelp: metadata is optional JSON on the key (returned by GET /v1/me).
create.externalHelp1: Provide External system, External user ID, and Email. The gateway will match an existing user by that pair or create one (email stored on users.email; required on first create). If the user already exists, the request email is not used to overwrite the stored email.
create.externalHelp2: New users from this path start with zero budget until you set a plan on Users.

placeholders.userEmail: Stored on users.email when creating via external identity

help.userEmail: Required for match-or-create. Not used to overwrite email when the external pair already exists.
help.metadata: Stored on the key and returned by GET /v1/me. Leave empty for none.
help.editMetadata: Full JSON stored on this key; cleared if empty. Shown on GET /v1/me.

titles.model: Gateway model name, protocol, and route group
titles.route: Provider, upstream model, and provider key
titles.cost: Standard (row 1), Charged × vs standard (row 2), Metered × vs standard (row 3)
titles.rowDetail: Click to show or hide pricing audit and request / usage bodies

timing.gatewayDesc: Auth, model routing, budget and circuit checks before upstream dispatch.
timing.upstreamDesc: Dispatch plus provider/key attempts until the selected upstream response headers arrive.
timing.headersDesc: The final selected provider fetch until headers return.
timing.reasoningDesc: Request start to first parsed reasoning/thinking chunk.
timing.ttftContentDesc: Request start to first parsed content token/chunk.
timing.streamDesc: Selected upstream headers to stream end, usage collection, cancel, or timeout.

auditLogs.title: Audit Logs
auditLogs.subtitle: User audit trail (user_audit_logs) — spent / budget_max are derived from before/after user snapshots; legacy / extra JSON from change_payload is appended below when present (no separate Metadata column).
auditLogs.empty: No audit logs match the filters.

modelUsage.title: Model Usage
modelUsage.subtitle: Usage and cost by model and route group

providerUsage.title: Provider Usage
providerUsage.subtitle: Usage and cost by upstream provider

userUsage.title: User Usage
userUsage.subtitle: Usage and cost by request log email; budget columns come from users (JOIN on user_id).

analytics.noProviderUsageForModel: No provider usage for this model in the selected range.
analytics.noModelUsageForProvider: No model usage for this provider in the selected range.
analytics.noModelUsageForUser: No model usage for this user in the selected range.

config.title: System config
config.subtitle: Key-value settings shared by the {product} proxy and this admin app (no duplicate env vars)

masterKey.title: Admin API master key
masterKey.description: Bearer secret for Authorization on /api/admin/* (this console and server-side callers). Set the same value as GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY on any external integrator that calls the Gateway Admin API.
masterKey.label: Master key

businessTimezone.title: Business timezone
businessTimezone.description: IANA name (e.g. Asia/Shanghai). Controls the calendar day for free-tier daily quota and "today" stats on the dashboard. Invalid values fall back to UTC on the Gateway.

billingCurrency.title: Billing currency
billingCurrency.description: Only USD or CNY. Applies to pricing_profile unit prices and api_keys budget fields (per-million-token pricing). Exposed on GET /v1/me as billing_currency.

errorWebhooks.title: Proxy error webhooks
errorWebhooks.description: When the Proxy persists a row with api_key_request_logs.status = error (after billing write succeeds), it optionally POSTs to the configured robot URLs. Leave a field empty to disable that channel.
errorWebhooks.urlsNote: URLs contain webhook secrets; use Hide before screen sharing if needed.

addKey.title: Add config key
addKey.description: Upsert any other system_config key. Keys reserved for the dedicated sections on this page cannot be added here.

errors.useBusinessTimezoneSection: Use the "Business timezone" section for BUSINESS_TIMEZONE.
errors.useBillingCurrencySection: Use the "Billing currency" section for BILLING_CURRENCY.
errors.useMasterKeySection: Use the "Admin API master key" section for MASTER_KEY.
errors.useWebhooksSection: Use the "Proxy error webhooks" section for ALERT_WEBHOOK_WECOM_URL / ALERT_WEBHOOK_FEISHU_URL.
errors.useToolsSection: Use Tools → Configuration for WEB_SEARCH_PROVIDER / WEB_SEARCH_API_KEY / WEB_SEARCH_COST.

config.title: Tools
config.subtitle: Configure product tools under /v1/tools/* (engine, API key, unit cost). Settings are stored in system_config and share the user chat budget.

webSearch.title: Web search
webSearch.description: Powers POST /v1/tools/web-search. Choose an implemented engine only—free text is rejected. Leave the API key empty and save to clear it (endpoint returns 503 until set again).
webSearch.apiKeyHint: Required for the tool to accept traffic. Cleared keys fall back to nothing (no env).

providerGuide.title: Search engine guide
providerGuide.subtitle: Index / sources and when to pick each engine. Not a direct Baidu or Google API.
providerGuide.disclaimer: None of these call Baidu or Google official search APIs. Each uses its own or partner web index; result URLs may still point at baidu.com, google.com, etc.

bocha.summary: AI-oriented Chinese search engine with its own large web index (positioned as a domestic Bing Search API alternative).
bocha.sources: Hundreds of millions to ~10B web pages plus partner content (news, encyclopedia, short video, weather, academic, etc.). Not Baidu/Google.
bocha.bestFor: China-region users, Chinese queries, domestic compliance.

tavily.summary: LLM-oriented retrieval layer: search + extract + rank in one call. Built for agents/RAG, not a classic SERP dump.
tavily.sources: Own crawler plus third-party aggregation (often associated with independent indexes such as Brave). Not an official Google/Baidu API.
tavily.bestFor: International region, English research, agent workflows.

cleversee.summary: Alibaba CleverSee (开析) AI-native web search with mainland_china / global region modes.
cleversee.sources: Alibaba’s own AI search index. Docs do not claim Baidu or Google backends. Gateway default is mainland_china; domain allow-lists switch to global.
cleversee.bestFor: When you need an explicit China vs global region switch on Alibaba Cloud.

tencent_wsa.summary: Tencent Cloud Web Search API (WSA), officially based on Sogou Search.
tencent_wsa.sources: Open web via Sogou plus Tencent ecosystem (Tencent News, Sogou Baike, Penguin accounts). Higher tiers add VR/authority verticals.
tencent_wsa.bestFor: China Chinese web as an alternative to Bocha; strong Sogou/Tencent content mix.

invocations.title: Tool invocations
invocations.subtitle: Calls billed as provider octafuse-tools (model_id tool:*). Click a row to inspect query and result summary. Same underlying rows as Request Logs.
invocations.empty: No tool invocations in this range.

detail.noListResults: No list items to show; switch to JSON if a raw payload exists.
detail.noResponseStored: No response summary stored (older rows only kept the query, or the call failed before results).
detail.hint: Response summaries are truncated for ops review; full chat transcripts stay on the client.

errors.invalidWebSearchProvider: Search engine must be one of the implemented options.
errors.invalidWebSearchCost: Cost must be a non-negative number.

tiersEditor.imageHint: Token rates billed from upstream Images usage × these $/1M rates. Route factors apply on top.
tiersEditor.footer: {currency}/1M; by input_tokens (last row open-ended). No tiers on save → null (bill 0).
tiersEditor.footerImage: Unit: {currency}/1M tokens · Text Input / Cached Text / Image Input / Cached Image Input / Image Output. Last row open-ended.
tiersEditor.lastTierOpenEnded: Last tier is open-ended (JSON upto: null); not editable

readOnlyTable.unitFooter: Unit: {unit} / 1M tokens. Tier basis: input_tokens / request.
